short_definition: The declarative watch object that names what signal, predicate, or cadence should wake or gate a Site Operating Loop.
description: WatchDefinition is the first-class semantic object for the watch side of a Site Operating Loop. It stays separate from the loop runtime host, the loop module body, and any one bounded Loop Run so that wakeup semantics remain inspectable and reusable.
boundaries: Not a single Loop Run or emitted object.

authority.notes: The package owns generic loop runtime semantics; the watch definition names the wake/gate condition.

examples: timer, webhook, or mailbox arrival watch

confidence.basis: The Site Operating Loop docs already distinguish loop policy, triggers, and bounded runs; the watch object names the wake/gate side of that split.
